moreContext: Miami's sushi scene has evolved significantly over the past decade, expanding beyond basic strip-mall sushi spots to include high-end omakase counters and fusion concepts that blend Japanese techniques with local ingredients and Latin influences. Food media guides like Eater's roundups have become essential resources as dining options proliferate, helping readers cut through noise and identify standout establishments. The sushi category remains one of the most competitive segments in upscale dining, with new concepts regularly launching to capture market share.

whatHappening: Miami Food Pug is publishing its weekly roundup of South Florida food news and events for May 22, 2026. The publication compiles food-related stories and local news that didn't make it into earlier coverage during the week.
whoInvolved: Miami Food Pug is a South Florida food and dining publication that curates local food news, restaurant updates, and culinary events. The outlet serves the Miami food community with weekly roundups and ongoing coverage of the regional dining scene.
whyMatters: Weekly food news roundups help local diners and food industry professionals stay informed about restaurant openings, closings, events, and trends in the region. moreContext: Miami Food Pug established a Friday tradition of compiling food-related news that didn't fit into the site's regular publication schedule throughout the week. This format allows the outlet to capture announcements, events, and stories that still merit reader attention despite arriving late in the editorial cycle. The South Florida food scene is particularly active, with constant restaurant developments, chef movements, and dining events making comprehensive coverage challenging. These weekly roundups serve as a catch-all mechanism for community-relevant content while maintaining editorial standards.

title: A Day in Sunset Harbour Miami
summary: Sunset Harbour Miami is unique. It is one of Miami Beach’s most popular, vibrant, and walkable neighborhoods. Located on the quieter bay side of South Beach, it trades the chaotic tourist energy of Ocean Drive for a chic, wellness-focused, local lifestyle. It packs a big…
whatHappening: Sunset Harbour is establishing itself as Miami Beach's premier wellness-focused neighborhood. The bayside district is drawing locals and visitors seeking upscale dining, shopping, and lifestyle experiences away from the crowded Ocean Drive tourist scene.
whoInvolved: Sunset Harbour encompasses multiple local businesses, restaurants, and wellness establishments that collectively define the neighborhood's character. Miami Curated, the publication covering the story, specializes in highlighting Miami's cultural and lifestyle destinations.
whyMatters: The rise of Sunset Harbour reflects a broader shift in Miami tourism and lifestyle preferences toward curated, wellness-oriented experiences over mass-market tourism. This trend supports local businesses and positions Miami Beach as a destination for affluent travelers seeking authenticity and health-conscious living.
moreContext: Sunset Harbour occupies Miami Beach's bay side, a traditionally quieter area compared to the party-centric Ocean Drive on the Atlantic coast. The neighborhood has evolved into a walkable district featuring upscale retail, farm-to-table restaurants, spas, and fitness studios that appeal to health-conscious residents and visitors. This transformation reflects Miami's broader repositioning as a lifestyle and wellness destination rather than solely a beach party hub. The neighborhood's emphasis on local culture and wellness aligns with post-pandemic travel trends favoring outdoor spaces, fitness amenities, and community-oriented experiences.

title: Taste the Gables is Back with 75 Restaurants
summary: The City of Coral Gables’ popular restaurant month, Taste the Gables, returns this July with its most expansive lineup to date. More than 75 restaurants, ranging from Michelin-recognized destinations to neighborhood favorites and varied cuisines, are participating, showcasing…
whatHappening: Taste the Gables, Coral Gables' signature restaurant month, is returning this July with 75 participating restaurants—the largest roster in the event's history. The lineup spans Michelin-recognized fine dining establishments to casual neighborhood spots across diverse cuisines.
whoInvolved: The City of Coral Gables is organizing the event, partnering with restaurants ranging from upscale destinations to local favorites throughout the municipality. The participating venues represent the breadth of dining options in South Florida's most prestigious restaurant district.
whyMatters: The expanded event drives foot traffic and revenue to local restaurants during summer, historically a slower dining season. The showcase elevates Coral Gables' reputation as a premier culinary destination while giving diners affordable access to high-end and neighborhood restaurants through special menus and pricing.
moreContext: Taste the Gables is an annual promotional event designed to highlight the city's robust restaurant scene and attract both locals and tourists. The program typically features special prix-fixe menus and curated dining experiences at participating venues. The expansion to 75 restaurants reflects growing interest in the event and the continued strength of Coral Gables' dining economy post-pandemic. Similar restaurant months occur in major cities nationwide, serving as proven marketing tools for culinary communities.

title: New Restaurant Reviews + Eating News – Part 2
summary: More new restaurant reviews, vinyl nights, Persian happy hour with delish light bites, restaurant closings and more in Part 2 of eating news in June. For Part 1, click here.
whatHappening: Miami's restaurant scene is buzzing with new openings, special dining events, and closures this June. The city is seeing vinyl nights, Persian happy hour specials with appetizers, and fresh restaurant reviews across the market.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated, a local food and lifestyle publication, is covering the developments happening across Miami's independent and established restaurant community. The coverage features various restaurant owners and operators launching new concepts and events.
whyMatters: These dining trends reveal Miami's evolving food culture and consumer appetite for experiential dining beyond traditional meals. Restaurant closures and openings signal shifting market dynamics, while special events like vinyl nights and themed happy hours show how venues are competing for diners through entertainment and value pricing.
moreContext: Miami's restaurant landscape has increasingly embraced hybrid experiences combining food with live entertainment and cultural programming. The June coverage is part of an ongoing series (Part 2 indicates continuing coverage), suggesting sustained momentum in the local food scene. Persian cuisine and themed happy hours reflect Miami's diverse demographic and growing interest in global cuisines beyond traditional offerings. Strategic timing of happy hours and special events during peak summer dining season helps restaurants manage seasonal fluctuations and build customer loyalty.

title: New Restaurant Reviews + More Eating News: Part 1
summary: Reviews of new restaurant openings including Casa Tua Cucina Wynwood, "Meet me at Milos "spritz and aperitivo special, a Dubai chewy cookie, new top spot for coffee lovers with a Norwegian dessert, first Cuban restaurant to get a Michelin star and a bundle for non alcoholic…
whatHappening: Miami's dining scene is experiencing a wave of new restaurant openings and culinary innovations. From Italian cuisine in Wynwood to cocktail bars and specialty coffee shops, multiple establishments are launching with distinct concepts and offerings.
whoInvolved: Miami Cuired is covering diverse restaurant operators including Casa Tua Cucina in Wynwood, Milos (known for spritz and aperitivo offerings), and a Cuban restaurant that has achieved Michelin star status. These establishments represent both established restaurant groups expanding into new concepts and individual operators entering the market.
whyMatters: These openings signal Miami's continued strength as a food and beverage destination, attracting both diners seeking new experiences and investors betting on the local restaurant market. The achievement of a Michelin star for a Cuban restaurant is particularly significant as it validates the cuisine's elevation in fine dining circles and raises the profile of Miami's culinary identity.
moreContext: Miami has increasingly positioned itself as a premier dining hub over the past decade, with growing international chef interest and investment in the market. The diversity of concepts launching—from Italian to Middle Eastern influences (Dubai cookies) to Scandinavian-inspired desserts—reflects broader trends of global culinary fusion and the city's appeal to multicultural audiences. Cuban cuisine's Michelin recognition marks a milestone for a cuisine deeply rooted in Miami's cultural identity, historically underrepresented in fine dining establishments. The emphasis on non-alcoholic options and aperitivo culture indicates shifting consumer preferences toward lower-alcohol and health-conscious dining experiences.

title: A Traditional Italian Fave Made Even Better: Il Mulino
summary: Il Mulino New York located at Acqualina Resort, the Five Star beachfront property which recently celebrated its 20th anniversary, has always been one of my favorite traditional Italian restaurants in Miami. It’s authentic, gives good quality/value, the food is reliably very…
whatHappening: Il Mulino New York, a traditional Italian restaurant located at the Acqualina Resort in Miami, continues to deliver authentic Italian cuisine at reliable quality. The restaurant maintains its reputation as a favorite among diners seeking genuine Italian dining experiences.
whoInvolved: Il Mulino New York operates at Acqualina Resort, a Five Star beachfront property in Miami that recently marked its 20th anniversary. The restaurant is known for authentic Italian cuisine with strong quality-to-value positioning.
whyMatters: Il Mulino's sustained reputation matters for Miami's fine dining landscape, reinforcing the market for high-quality, traditional Italian restaurants in luxury hospitality settings. The restaurant's longevity and consistent quality demonstrate enduring demand for authentic Italian cuisine in the competitive Miami dining scene.
moreContext: Il Mulino has established itself as a reliable destination for traditional Italian dining in Miami, distinguishing itself through authenticity rather than trendy innovation. Acqualina Resort, a luxury beachfront property, provides an upscale venue for the restaurant's operations. The resort's recent 20th anniversary milestone underscores the longevity of both the property and its dining offerings. Miami's dining market continues to feature strong competition among Italian restaurants, where Il Mulino maintains its position through consistent execution and value proposition.

title: Miami’s New Modern Mexican Hotspot—What You Need to Know
summary: We went to the Mexican Miami less than three weeks after opening and it was buzzy and many of its seats taken with individuals at the bar, small groups at high top round tables, and others headed to the private rooms celebrating a special occasion. The word has gotten out…
whatHappening: A new Modern Mexican restaurant has opened in Miami and is already generating significant buzz just three weeks after launch. The venue is drawing crowds to its bar seating, high-top tables, and private dining spaces.
whoInvolved: Mexican Miami is the new establishment making waves in Miami's dining scene. The restaurant was reviewed by Miami Curated, a local food and lifestyle publication.
whyMatters: The strong early traffic suggests the restaurant has tapped into Miami's appetite for modern Mexican cuisine and upscale dining experiences. Early momentum like this often determines whether new restaurants gain staying power in a competitive market.
moreContext: Miami has a rich Mexican culinary heritage, but the market continues to see demand for elevated, contemporary takes on traditional cuisine. The restaurant's design features versatile spaces—casual bar seating for walk-ins, communal high-top areas for small groups, and private rooms for celebrations—indicating a strategy to capture different dining occasions and customer segments. This multi-format approach has become increasingly popular among new restaurant concepts seeking to maximize revenue and appeal across demographics.
